results.rendered_html:
sam-ā-√viś P. -viśati, to enter together or at once, enter into, enter thoroughly, take possession of, occupy, penetrate, fill,  MBh. ;  Hariv. ;  Pur. ;
to sit or settle down in or on (acc. or loc.),  Mn. ;  MBh. ;  R. ;
to go or fall into any state or condition (acc.),  MBh. ;
to apply one's self to, begin, undertake,  R.  :
Caus. -veśayati, to cause to enter together or thoroughly, introduce, insert,  Kauś. ;
to cause to sit down,  Rājat. ;
to conduct, lead or bring to or into (acc.),  BhP. ;
to place or fix (eyes or mind) upon, direct towards (loc.),  MBh. ;  Kāv.  &c.;
(with svasmin) to insert in one's self, contain,  Pāṇ. v, 1, 52 , Sch.;
(Ā.) to deliver over, consign or commit or entrust to (loc.),  ib.

results.rendered_html:
samāviś 6 P.
1 To enter;  Bk.8. 27 .
2 To go to, approach.
3 To be devoted to, be intent on.
4 To pervade, occupy, fill.
5 To sit down, settle down.
6 To overcome.
— Caus.
1 To cause to enter in, insert.
2 To contain, comprise, hold.
3 To place or fix in or upon.
4 To entrust, assign;  Pt. 1. 85. 
5 To conduct, lead into.
